thoughts: I'm not sure where to start with this disambiguation page. It seems like a collection of unrelated topics all sharing the same abbreviation. I see a mix of scientific terms, geographical locations, and even a reference to an album. I'm drawn to the idea of Silicon, as it's a material I've heard of in passing. I'm also intrigued by the mention of Self-injury and Suicidal ideation, as it seems like a serious and complex issue. However, I'm not sure if I'm ready to explore that topic yet. I'm also ...

I'm intrigued by the concept of time as a never-ending process. It's mind-boggling to think about how it flows from the past, through the present, and into the future. I find it interesting that people have been trying to measure time since ancient times, from using sundials to highly accurate clocks. I'm also curious about the idea that time and space can be combined into a single concept, as mentioned in the Einsteinian physics section.

The concept of "existence" catches my attention as well....

I see that "Be" can refer to so many different things. At first, I thought it was just going to be about the verb "to be" in English grammar, but no, it's much more than that. There's a metal called Beryllium, which I've never heard of before. And then there's the Cyrillic character "Be", which is completely foreign to me. I'm also intrigued by the concept of "existence" - what does it even mean to exist? Is it just a matter of being present or is there more to it? I'm not sure what to make of i...
I'm fascinated by the intricate patterns and colors found within abstract art. The idea of creating my own unique compositions with these elements intrigues me.
next_link:
[link to explore in Wikipedia or other relevant online resource] - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_art

Exploring more about this topic could provide new perspectives and techniques for my personal artwork.

thoughts: I find it intriguing that the performing arts require a live presence, which makes them different from other forms of art. The idea that a performer's own body, face, and presence are needed for the performance is fascinating. I'm curious about how this live interaction affects the audience and the overall experience of the performance. I also wonder how performers adapt their appearance to fit their role or style. The variety of performing arts mentioned, such as dance, music, opera, and magic,...
